tagline: 3,000+ utility interconnection rules, all in one place
hero_sub: Aggregated interconnection rules for 3,000+ US utilities. Never again wonder about capacity limits, net metering policies, timelines, costs, and approval cycles.
pains: Different rules for every utility—hunting through PDFs and policy docs wastes hours
Capacity limits and net metering policies change constantly. Manual tracking means missing updates
No way to quickly compare costs, timelines, and feasibility across utilities before quoting a job

features.title: 3,000+ utility database
features.desc: All major US utility interconnection rules in one place—no more digging through websites

features.title: Real-time policy updates
features.desc: Auto-alerts for changes in net metering, capacity, and fees

features.title: Side-by-side comparison
features.desc: Compare rules and costs across utilities instantly

features.title: Installation workflow guide
features.desc: Step-by-step: required forms, costs, and approval timelines

how.desc: Get your utility's interconnection rules instantly
Capacity limits, net metering, fees, and approval process at a glance
Compare rules and costs to decide whether to take the job and set your price

proof: Built with solar associations and equipment makers. Currently in beta with installers validating strong product demand
cta_sub: Completely free. Help us refine the product. Get permanent discounts during beta.

faq.q: Where does this data come from?
faq.a: We compile official state utility policy documents, rate schedules, and net metering rules—then manually verify for accuracy
Critical changes get pushed in real-time. Full database audit monthly to keep everything current

faq.q: Can you integrate with my quoting system?
faq.a: Yes—API and Zapier integration available. Plug into your workflow without changing your tools

disclaimer: This platform is an information reference tool, not official policy interpretation. Always consult your state utility's official resources and a licensed electrical engineer before applying any rules. When in doubt, defer to official documents and professional guidance.
